segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: OK, we'll think about that.
segments.zh: 好的，我们会考虑一下。现在我们是不是……

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: Now shall we ...

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In this series of lectures about the history of weather forecasting, I'll start by examining its early history – that'll be the subject of today's talk.
segments.zh: 在这一系列关于天气预报历史的讲座中，我将首先探讨它的早期历史——这也是今天讲座的主题。那么，我们从几千年前开始讲起。大多数古代文化都有天气神，像洪水这样的天气灾难在许多创世神话中扮演了重要角色。通常，天气被归因于神明的反复无常，这一点从各种文化中众多的天气神就能看出。例如，埃及的太阳神拉，以及北欧的雷神托尔。许多古代文明发展出了诸如舞蹈之类的仪式，以祈求天气神的眷顾。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: OK, so we'll start by going back thousands of years.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Most ancient cultures had weather gods, and weather catastrophes, such as floods, played an important role in many creation myths.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Generally, weather was attributed to the whims of the gods, as the wide range of weather gods in various cultures shows.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For instance, there's the Egyptian sun god Ra, and Thor, the Norse god of thunder and lightning.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Many ancient civilisations developed rites such as dances in order to make the weather gods look kindly on them.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But the weather was of daily importance: observing the skies and drawing the correct conclusions from these observations was really important, in fact their survival depended on it.
segments.zh: 但天气对日常生活至关重要：观察天空并从这些观察中得出正确结论非常重要，事实上他们的生存依赖于它。人们何时开始观察天空不得而知，但大约在公元前650年，巴比伦人根据对云和其他现象的观察，做出了第一批短期天气预报。中国人也识别出了天气模式，到公元前300年，天文学家已经制定了一种历法，将一年分为24个节气，每个节气都与不同的天气现象相关联。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It isn't known when people first started to observe the skies, but at around 650 BC, the Babylonians produced the first short-range weather forecasts, based on their observations of clouds and other phenomena.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The Chinese also recognised weather patterns, and by 300 BC, astronomers had developed a calendar which divided the year into 24 festivals, each associated with a different weather phenomenon.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The ancient Greeks were the first to develop a more scientific approach to explaining the weather.
segments.zh: 古希腊人率先发展出更科学的方法来解释天气。公元前4世纪的哲学家兼科学家亚里士多德的著作尤其值得注意，他的观点主导了近2000年。公元前340年，他写了一本书，试图解释雨、云、风和风暴的形成。他还描述了诸如晕——即太阳、月亮和亮星周围的光环——以及彗星等天体现象。他的许多观察惊人地准确。例如，他认为热量能使水蒸发。但他也得出了一些错误的结论，比如风是地球呼出的。像这样的错误从文艺复兴时期开始被纠正。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The work of the philosopher and scientist Aristotle, in the fourth century BC, is especially noteworthy, as his ideas held sway for nearly 2,000 years.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In 340 BC, he wrote a book in which he attempted to account for the formation of rain, clouds, wind and storms.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: He also described celestial phenomena such as haloes – that is, bright circles of light around the sun, the moon and bright stars – and comets.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Many of his observations were surprisingly accurate.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For example, he believed that heat could cause water to evaporate.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But he also jumped to quite a few wrong conclusions, such as that winds are breathed out by the Earth.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Errors like this were rectified from the Renaissance onwards.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For nearly 2,000 years, Aristotle's work was accepted as the chief authority on weather theory.
segments.zh: 近2000年来，亚里士多德的著作被接受为天气理论的主要权威。不过，与此同时，在中世纪，天气观察以谚语的形式流传，比如“晚霞行千里，朝霞不出门”。正如当代气象学家所发现的，许多谚语基于非常好的观察，并且是准确的。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Alongside this, though, in the Middle Ages weather observations were passed on in the form of proverbs, such as 'Red sky at night, shepherd's delight; red sky in the morning, shepherd's warning'.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Many of these are based on very good observations and are accurate, as contemporary meteorologists have discovered.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For centuries, any attempt to forecast the weather could only be based on personal observations, but in the fifteenth century scientists began to see the need for instruments.
segments.zh: 几个世纪以来，任何预测天气的尝试都只能基于个人观察，但在15世纪，科学家开始意识到需要仪器。在那之前，唯一可用的仪器是风向标——用来确定风向——以及早期版本的雨量计。最早发明的仪器之一是15世纪的湿度计，用于测量湿度。这是众多促进天气预报发展的发明之一。1592年，意大利科学家兼发明家伽利略发明了世界上第一个温度计。他的学生托里拆利后来发明了气压计，使人们能够测量大气压力。1648年，法国哲学家帕斯卡证明了气压随海拔升高而降低。这一发现于1686年由英国天文学家哈雷证实；哈雷也是第一个绘制信风图的人。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Until then, the only ones available were weather vanes – to determine the wind direction – and early versions of rain gauges.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One of the first, invented in the fifteenth century, was a hygrometer, which measured humidity.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This was one of many inventions that contributed to the development of weather forecasting.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In 1592, the Italian scientist and inventor Galileo developed the world's first thermometer.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: His student Torricelli later invented the barometer, which allowed people to measure atmospheric pressure.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In 1648, the French philosopher Pascal proved that pressure decreases with altitude.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This discovery was verified by English astronomer Halley in 1686; and Halley was also the first person to map trade winds.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This increasing ability to measure factors related to weather helped scientists to understand the atmosphere and its processes better, and they started collecting weather observation data systematically.
segments.zh: 这种测量与天气相关因素的能力不断增强，帮助科学家更好地理解大气及其过程，他们开始系统地收集天气观测数据。18世纪，科学家兼政治家本杰明·富兰克林特别进行了关于电和闪电的研究，但他也对天气非常感兴趣，并一生大部分时间都在研究天气。正是富兰克林发现了风暴通常自西向东移动。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In the eighteenth century, the scientist and politician Benjamin Franklin carried out work on electricity and lightning in particular, but he was also very interested in weather and studied it throughout most of his life.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It was Franklin who discovered that storms generally travel from west to east.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In addition to new meteorological instruments, other developments contributed to our understanding of the atmosphere.
segments.zh: 除了新的气象仪器，其他发展也促进了我们对大气的理解。不同地点的人们开始记录数据，19世纪中叶电报的发明使得这些记录得以汇总。这导致了19世纪末第一批气象服务的出现。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: People in different locations began to keep records, and in the mid-nineteenth century, the invention of the telegraph made it possible for these records to be collated.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This led, by the end of the nineteenth century, to the first weather services.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It was not until the early twentieth century that mathematics and physics became part of meteorology, and we'll continue from that point next week.
segments.zh: 直到20世纪初，数学和物理才成为气象学的一部分，下周我们将从这一点继续讲起。

items.prompt: many cultures invented 31 and other ceremonies to make the weather gods friendly

pairs.note: rites 和 ceremonies 都表示‘仪式’，是同义替换。
developed 在这里表示‘发明、创造’，与 invented 同义。

pairs.p: make the weather gods look kindly on them
pairs.note: friendly 被替换为 look kindly on，意思相同，但表达方式不同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到‘Many ancient civilisations developed rites such as dances in order to make the weather gods look kindly on them’，其中 rites 对应 ceremonies，developed 对应 invented，make ... look kindly on them 对应 make ... friendly，因此答案 dances 直接出现在定位句中。

items.prompt: people needed to observe and interpret the sky to ensure their 32

pairs.note: 题干说'确保他们的...'，原文说'他们的生存依赖于它'，意思相同，只是表达方式不同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'observing the skies and drawing the correct conclusions...their survival depended on it'，与题干'观察和解读天空以确保他们的...'对应，答案就是'survival'。

items.prompt: around 650 BC, Babylonians started forecasting, using weather phenomena such as 33

pairs.note: 原文直接提到'clouds'作为现象之一，与题干中的'weather phenomena such as'对应。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到Babylonians基于对clouds和其他现象的观察进行预报，与题干中'using weather phenomena such as clouds'一致。

items.prompt: by 300 BC, the Chinese had a calendar made up of a number of 34 connected with the weather

pairs.note: 原文直接给出数字24，对应题干中的'a number of'，表示数量。

pairs.p: each associated with a different weather phenomenon
pairs.note: 原文用'each associated with a different weather phenomenon'解释'connected with the weather'，是并列关系。

pairs.p: calendar which divided the year into
pairs.note: 原文'calendar which divided the year into'与题干'calendar made up of'同义，都表示日历由...组成。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'by 300 BC, astronomers had developed a calendar which divided the year into 24 festivals, each associated with a different weather phenomenon'，其中'24 festivals'对应题干中的'a number of'和'connected with the weather'，因此答案是festivals。

pairs.note: 原文中直接出现 haloes，与题干相同。
题干中 35 是题号，答案 comets 在原文中直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中 'haloes and 35' 提示答案与 haloes 并列，原文中 'haloes ... and comets' 直接给出答案 comets。

items.prompt: many proverbs, e.g. about the significance of the colour of the 36, passed on accurate information.

pairs.note: 原文直接出现'Red sky'，与题干'colour of the sky'对应。

pairs.p: are based on very good observations and are accurate
pairs.note: 题干说'passed on accurate information'，原文用'are based on very good observations and are accurate'同义转述。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问谚语传递了关于什么颜色的准确信息，定位句提到'Red sky'，所以答案应为'sky'。

items.prompt: 15th century: scientists recognised value of 37 for the first time

pairs.p: scientists began to see the need for
pairs.note: recognised value of 被替换为 began to see the need for，意思相近，都是意识到某物的重要性。
37 指代 instruments，原文直接出现 instruments。

paraphrase.explain: 题干说15世纪科学家首次认识到37的价值，定位句提到15世纪科学家开始意识到需要 instruments，因此37对应 instruments。

pairs.note: invented 和 developed 是同义替换，都表示'发明'。
题干中的数字38对应答案thermometer，原文直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句明确提到'Galileo developed the world's first thermometer'，其中'developed'替换了题干中的'invented'，'thermometer'就是答案。

items.prompt: 18th century: Franklin identified the movement of 39

pairs.note: 题干说'storms的运动'，原文说'storms generally travel'，travel替换movement。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到Franklin发现风暴通常从西向东移动，其中'storms generally travel'对应题干中的'movement of storms'。

items.prompt: 19th century: data from different locations could be sent to the same place by 40

pairs.note: 19th century 和 nineteenth century 是同义表达，都指19世纪。

pairs.q: data from different locations could be sent to the same place
pairs.p: made it possible for these records to be collated
pairs.note: 题干说数据被发送到同一地点，原文说记录被整理，意思相近。
题干中的40是题号，实际答案是telegraph，原文直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到19世纪中期电报的发明使记录得以整理，对应题干中19世纪数据被发送到同一地点，因此答案telegraph。
